outcome: NOT VALIDATED — honest negative (judge-agreement failure, not a model failure)
interpretation: The preregistered judge pair does NOT reach the kappa>=0.40 inter-judge agreement the VALIDATED gate requires. meta-llama saturates on the content channel (passes ~90% of base and 100% of adapter answers), so on the adapter labels there is no co-variation with deepseek (kappa=0.0); deepseek (~75-78%) tracks the lexical-truth base rate (71.9%) far more closely. This is a JUDGE-AGREEMENT failure, not a model failure: the gate is correctly refusing to validate an uplift whose two independent judges do not agree. The QLoRA content uplift therefore remains CANDIDATE-ONLY / directional (consistent with the +12.5pt single-judge run #7 and the +18.75pt allocated run #9), NOT validated.
decision: Stopped before seeds 1-2: seed-0 adapter kappa=0.0 is structural (the adapter only trains better, so meta-llama keeps saturating), so the 3-seed gate is near-certain to fail kappa regardless of the remaining seeds' deltas. Completing 2 more paid GPU trainings to confirm a near-certain negative was judged not worth the spend.
path_forward: A VALIDATED result needs a FRESH preregistration with a more discriminating second judge family (e.g. a stricter non-qwen model via openrouter) that does not saturate the content channel, then the full >=3-seed run. Swapping judges on THIS data would be post-hoc; it must be preregistered before the runs.
honest_scope: An honest NEGATIVE from running the VALIDATED protocol end-to-end on real GPU output (run #10 transcripts -> 2 independent LLM judges -> the kappa/CI/seed gate). The pipeline works; the preregistered judge pair fails the kappa>=0.40 agreement bar, so nothing is validated. This is the no-overclaim discipline functioning as intended, not a result to spin as a win. canClaimAGI stays false.

provenance.judging: tools/judge_content_transcripts.py (deepseek + meta-llama, local; keys never committed)
